incident_summary: Freeze dryer HP-140 shelf temperature excursion during primary drying of batch B6285C. Shelf temp rose 3.3°C above validated maximum for 7.5 hours. 1733 vials potentially affected.
root_cause_detail: Shelf temperature controller PID settings reset to factory default after firmware update. Validation of PID settings not included in software change control.
corrective_action: Cycle aborted. PID settings restored and validated. Batch quarantined and assessed via appearance, moisture content, and reconstitution testing.
preventive_action: Software change control procedure updated to include PID settings verification post-update. Lyophiliser firmware updates require re-validation of temperature control before production use.
effectiveness_check: Zero shelf temperature excursions for 12 months post-implementation. Software change control compliance reviewed at quarterly audit.
